label: Purple Team Analyst
description: Bridges offensive and defensive security — conducting structured attack-detect-respond cycles, validating detection coverage, and improving defensive tooling through adversary simulation. Typically a senior role requiring 3+ years of combined red and blue experience.

paths.name: Standard Track
paths.rationale: BTL2 provides advanced blue team depth and is the recognized senior blue team cert. CWL CPTA adds structured purple team methodology with ATT&CK mapping and detection validation. GDAT from GIAC is the senior enterprise capstone — DoD-recognized adversary tactics coverage. Requires 2-4 years of combined offensive/defensive baseline.
pros: BTL2 is the recognized advanced blue team credential — direct purple team relevance
CWL CPTA is the only dedicated purple team cert with structured ATT&CK methodology
GDAT adds GIAC brand recognition and DoD-adjacent credibility
The combination covers offensive simulation, detection validation, and defensive response
cons: Requires 2-4 years offensive + defensive experience before starting
GDAT is expensive — employer funding recommended
Purple Team Analyst as a title may consolidate into Detection Engineering by 2027
Role is rare at junior level — most organizations hire senior practitioners into purple team

prerequisites.experience: 2-4 years of combined red team and blue team experience required. OSCP or BTL1 as minimum prior credentialing. This is not an entry-level path.

cert_sequence.notes: Blue Team Labs Level 2 — the advanced blue team cert covering threat hunting, advanced DFIR, malware triage, and enterprise incident response. ~$999. Prerequisite BTL1 experience recommended. Establishes the defensive depth that makes purple team work credible rather than superficial.

CWL Certified Purple Team Analyst v2 — structured attack-detect-respond cycles, ATT&CK framework mapping, detection rule validation, and offensive/defensive perspective integration. $199, practical instructor-evaluated exam. The only dedicated purple team cert — add during a CWL sale for maximum value.

GIAC Defending Advanced Threats — covers advanced adversary techniques and organizational defensive response. Associated with SANS SEC530. The GIAC brand and DoD-adjacent recognition is the primary value. Employer-funded recommended given SANS pricing (~$9,800 all-in). With BTL2 and CPTA foundations completed, GDAT deepens the enterprise defensive architecture context.

paths.name: Budget Track
paths.rationale: For practitioners who want purple team credentialing without GIAC investment. BTL2 + CWL CPTA + SecOps Group CBTeamerX covers the full purple team scope at under $2,000 total. CBTeamerX (launched January 2026) is the most advanced defensive practical exam in the SecOps Group catalog.
pros: Under $1,800 total — fraction of the GDAT track cost
CBTeamerX is a 7-hour multi-stage APT investigation — genuinely rigorous practical exam
All three certs are practical or lab-based
Lifetime validity on SecOps Group certs
cons: No GIAC brand — limits government/enterprise recognition
CBTeamerX is very new (January 2026) — building market presence
Portfolio of purple team exercise writeups remains essential

prerequisites.experience: 2+ years combined red/blue experience. OSCP or BTL1 recommended.

cert_sequence.notes: BTL2 — advanced blue team foundation. The defensive depth prerequisite for credible purple team work.
CWL CPTA v2 — the dedicated purple team cert. $199, add during a CWL sale.

SecOps Group CBTeamerX — 7-hour multi-stage APT investigation across on-prem AD and cloud. Launched January 2026. £400 (~$510). The most rigorous budget defensive practical exam available. With BTL2 and CPTA foundations this tests everything simultaneously.
